attacking_style: Pure flat sprint finisher who launches extremely late (100-300m) with explosive speed differential that requires minimal acceleration distance. Thrives in chaotic, disrupted sprints where positioning trumps organized leadouts.
team_tactics: Relies on team positioning control through finale rather than traditional high-speed leadout train. Team focuses on keeping him in top-10 positions through final 3km, but execution consistency varies—positioning errors repeat across multiple races.
terrain_preference: Flat, technical finales where late positioning and cornering awareness create advantages. Exposed and vulnerable on crosswind stages despite winning on coastal roads—struggles to stay with front groups when echelons form.
vulnerability: Loses crucial positions in technical sections inside final 3km (turns, crashes, compression). Crosswind splits consistently expose him despite team strength. Physical durability questionable—used barriers for balance after consecutive race days.
key_patterns: Extreme speed differential allows 100m maximum sprint duration versus competitors needing 250-300m (3 observations)
Positioning errors in final 3km repeatedly compromise sprint setup despite team control (3 instances)
Excels in disrupted/chaotic sprints without organized leadouts, showing tactical opportunism (2+ wins)
Crosswind vulnerability: caught behind splits or struggles physically despite team presence (2 observations)
Late-season form builder: performs better deeper into stage races after initial struggles
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 data shows season form building with winning acceleration distance increasing, though crosswind vulnerability and physical struggles after consecutive stages remain concerns from 2022 comeback
